daily_brief: The US Supreme Court delivered a significant blow to President Trump's immigration agenda on Tuesday, ruling 6-3 to uphold birthright citizenship and reject the administration's executive order targeting children of undocumented immigrants and temporary visitors [BBC, Globe and Mail, Al Jazeera]. Meanwhile, Trump's financial disclosures revealed he earned over $1.2 billion from cryptocurrency ventures in 2025, including $550 million from World Liberty Financial and $635 million from his meme coin [France 24, BBC, Le Monde]. The US lifted export restrictions on Anthropic's most powerful AI models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, restoring access for users in allied nations including New Zealand [RNZ, Le Monde]. In Myanmar, conflict monitoring group ACLED reported that deaths since the 2021 military coup have surpassed 100,000, marking it as Asia's deadliest active conflict [France 24]. Tensions remain high in South Africa as anti-immigration protests drew thousands of demonstrators, with authorities deploying police nationwide to prevent violence after weeks of unrest that has already displaced tens of thousands of foreign nationals [Guardian, Globe and Mail]. On the diplomatic front, Iran declined to meet directly with US envoys in Qatar, complicating ceasefire negotiations [Globe and Mail].

top_stories.headline: US Supreme Court Upholds Birthright Citizenship, Rejects Trump Executive Order
bullets: Supreme Court ruled 6-3 that children born in the US to undocumented immigrants or temporary visitors are citizens under the 14th Amendment
Chief Justice Roberts joined the liberal justices in finding Trump's executive order unconstitutional; Justice Kavanaugh found it violated federal law rather than the Constitution
Justices Thomas, Alito, and Gorsuch dissented; Thomas wrote a 91-page dissent arguing the court misinterpreted the 14th Amendment
Trump called the decision 'too bad' and urged Congress to pass legislation ending birthright citizenship

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized this as a 'major setback' for Trump and noted his 'tepid' response compared to usual standards

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on constitutional analysis and noted Trump personally attended oral arguments - first sitting president to do so

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted 'MAGA meltdown' reaction to the ruling

top_stories.headline: Trump Earned $1.2 Billion from Cryptocurrency Ventures in 2025, Disclosure Shows
bullets: Financial disclosure reveals $550 million from World Liberty Financial, co-founded by Trump's sons and the son of envoy Steve Witkoff
$635 million came from royalties related to the $TRUMP meme coin launched days before his January 2025 inauguration
Trump's personal fortune reportedly tripled from $2.3 billion to $6.5 billion between 2024 and 2026
White House denied any conflict of interest, stating Trump made the US 'the crypto capital of the world'

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Noted Trump is 'regularly accused of conflicts of interest' and detailed the timing of crypto investments

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Included White House denial and noted businesses are in trust managed by his sons

top_stories.headline: US Lifts Export Restrictions on Anthropic's Most Powerful AI Models
bullets: Department of Commerce removed export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, Anthropic's most advanced AI systems
The models had been restricted last month on security grounds, blocking access in allied nations including New Zealand
Claude Fable 5 is Anthropic's most powerful model cleared for general use; Mythos 5 is restricted to trusted companies and governments for cybersecurity purposes
Anthropic announced it would begin restoring access immediately

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Focused on implications for New Zealand users and noted tech expert criticism of the original ban

top_stories.headline: Myanmar Conflict Death Toll Surpasses 100,000 Since 2021 Coup
bullets: Armed Conflict Location & Event Data (ACLED) reports 100,114 conflict-related fatalities since the military coup in February 2021
The military ousted the elected government of Aung San Suu Kyi, triggering civil war between junta forces and pro-democracy guerrillas allied with ethnic minority armies
Analysts regard the five-year civil war as Asia's deadliest active conflict
No official death toll exists; estimates vary widely based on media reports of violence

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Included personal testimony from widow whose husband was killed in recent airstrike in Rakhine state

top_stories.headline: Mass Anti-Immigration Protests Sweep South Africa Amid Fears of Violence
bullets: Thousands demonstrated across South Africa against undocumented immigrants after activist groups set June 30 deadline for migrants to leave
At least four people have been killed in preceding weeks of anti-foreigner violence; tens of thousands have fled their homes
More than 25,000 migrants have been repatriated by various African governments; authorities deployed police and military assets nationwide
Government rejected the arbitrary deadline, stating only authorities can enforce immigration laws

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Extensive coverage of displaced migrants and personal stories of those fleeing violence

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Focused on government response and comparisons to 2008 anti-migrant riots that killed 62

additional_stories.headline: Iran refuses to meet US envoys in Qatar, complicating ceasefire negotiations over Strait of Hormuz
Indonesian tech entrepreneur and former education minister Nadiem Makarim sentenced to 10 years for corruption in Chromebook procurement case
World's oceans recorded hottest June ever as El Niño threatens to push temperatures to new records
Three-year-old boy rescued alive six days after Venezuela earthquakes that have killed nearly 2,000 people
Democratic socialist Melat Kiros defeats 15-term incumbent Diana DeGette in Colorado congressional primary
Six people shot dead at mothers and children center in Germany in custody dispute
South Korea exports surge past $100 billion for first time in June, driven by record semiconductor shipments
Germany's domestic intelligence agency reports far-right extremist numbers rose to 58,700 in 2025
US removes four Indian companies from Russia-related sanctions list
Canada signals no USMCA extension expected as trade deal enters annual review period
